{
  "purpose": "The script modifies a README.md file generated by documentation.js to clean up the Table of Contents and fix source code links with correct commit hashes and version tags.",
  "sources": "Reads the README.md file from disk using fs.readFileSync, and reads the package.json for version info.",
  "sinks": "Writes back to the same README.md file using fs.writeFileSync after modifications.",
  "flows": "Reads input from filesystem -> processes and manipulates README content -> writes updated content back to filesystem.",
  "anomalies": "No hardcoded credentials, backdoors, or malicious code patterns detected. The code performs straightforward text processing and file I/O.",
  "analysis": "The code loads package version info and README content. It iterates through each line, identifying section headings. When in the 'Table of Contents' section, it removes certain entries. Outside that section, it detects source code links matching a specific pattern and attempts to correct line number ranges and URLs, especially replacing commit hashes with version tags. It also updates GitHub blob URLs to include the current package version. The operations are purely content and URL string manipulations with no suspicious or malicious behaviors.",
  "conclusion": "The script is a standard documentation utility for cleaning and correcting links in README files. No malicious intent, backdoors, or malware are detected. It only performs text processing and file modifications related to documentation maintenance.",
  "confidence": 1,
  "obfuscated": 0,
  "malware": 0,
